# UC-22.1.2 · GDPR Data Subject Access Request Fulfillment Tracking

## Description

Measures DSAR ticket lifecycle from opened to closed against a 30-calendar-day SLA so privacy and audit teams can evidence timely handling of access, rectification, erasure, and portability requests.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident, snow:sc_req_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Install Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) with sc_req_item and incident inputs enabled; (2) align `cat_item`/`short_description` filters with your DSAR catalogue naming; (3) confirm timestamp format in `opened_at`/`closed_at` and adjust `strptime` format if needed; (4) schedule daily and alert on `open_breach`. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`, `sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, cat_item, opened_at, closed_at, state, short_description) or `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, category, opened_at, closed_at, short_description, priority).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Install Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) with sc_req_item and incident inputs enabled; (2) align `cat_item`/`short_description` filters with your DSAR catalogue naming; (3) confirm timestamp format in `opened_at`/`closed_at` and adjust `strptime` format if needed; (4) schedule daily and alert on `open_breach`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" OR sourcetype="snow:incident")
    (cat_item="*Subject Access*" OR short_description="*DSAR*" OR short_description="*data subject*")
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed_epoch=if(isnotnull(closed_at), strptime(closed_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null())
| eval age_days=round((now()-opened_epoch)/86400, 1)
| eval sla_met=if(isnotnull(closed_epoch) AND (closed_epoch-opened_epoch)<=2592000, "Met", "Missed")
| eval open_breach=if(isnull(closed_epoch) AND age_days>30, "Open_SLA_Breach", null())
| table _time, number, sourcetype, state, age_days, sla_met, open_breach, short_description
| sort - age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Data Subject Access Request Fulfillment Tracking (Art. 15-22)** — Measures DSAR ticket lifecycle from opened to closed against a 30-calendar-day SLA so privacy and audit teams can evidence timely handling of access, rectification, erasure, and portability requests.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, cat_item, opened_at, closed_at, state, short_description) or `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, category, opened_at, closed_at, short_description, priority). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:sc_req_item, snow:incident. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opened_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **closed_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_met** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **open_breach** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: _time, age_days, closed_epoch, number, open_breach, opened_epoch, short_description, sla_met.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare ticket and field counts with the ServiceNow ITSM or GRC console (same time range) and confirm synchronization fields and sourcetypes used by this search.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column chart (Met vs Missed), Time chart (DSAR volume), Table (open breaches), Single value (% within 30 days).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" OR sourcetype="snow:incident")
    (cat_item="*Subject Access*" OR short_description="*DSAR*" OR short_description="*data subject*")
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed_epoch=if(isnotnull(closed_at), strptime(closed_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null())
| eval age_days=round((now()-opened_epoch)/86400, 1)
| eval sla_met=if(isnotnull(closed_epoch) AND (closed_epoch-opened_epoch)<=2592000, "Met", "Missed")
| eval open_breach=if(isnull(closed_epoch) AND age_days>30, "Open_SLA_Breach", null())
| table _time, number, sourcetype, state, age_days, sla_met, open_breach, short_description
| sort - age_days
```

## Visualization

Column chart (Met vs Missed), Time chart (DSAR volume), Table (open breaches), Single value (% within 30 days). Key fields: `age_days`, `closed_epoch`, `number`, `open_breach`, `opened_epoch`, `short_description`.
